tips: Reading just 20 minutes a day exposes you to around 1.8 million words per year.
Books are the quietest and most constant of friends — and the wisest of counselors.
The more that you read, the more things you will know. The more you learn, the more places you'll go.
Reading literary fiction sharpens empathy and helps you understand others' emotions.
A calming bedtime reading routine signals your brain that it's time to sleep.
Public libraries grant you free access to the world's collective knowledge — use them!
Taking short notes while reading dramatically improves long-term retention.
Re-reading a beloved book often reveals hidden details you missed the first time.
Regular reading slows cognitive decline and keeps your mind sharp as you age.
Reading authors from different cultures expands your worldview beyond your own experience.
Just 6 minutes of reading can reduce stress levels by up to 68%, studies show.
Setting a clear reading goal — even just one book a month — builds a powerful habit.
Reading aloud to children, even briefly each day, builds vocabulary and love for stories.
Joining a book club transforms reading into a social, discussion-rich adventure.
